title: Invoke Ledger operations non-interactively
description: Provide deterministic JSON-oriented process input, output, diagnostics, and error behavior for the provider-neutral public command contract. Implements G5 and UC-LEDGER-006 under C2, C6, and C14.
rationale: Agents must distinguish structured outcomes from diagnostics and must never parse prose to know whether a financial mutation succeeded.
story_want: agents to invoke commands through a stable process contract
story_so_that: automation can handle success and failure deterministically
acceptance_criteria: Given a structured public operation, when valid JSON input is supplied through the documented non-interactive input path, then exactly one structured result is written to stdout and the process exits with the documented success code.

Given an expected validation, conflict, lifecycle, compatibility, cursor, not-found, ambiguous-match, stale-projection, or review-required failure, when the operation completes, then one machine-readable module-scoped error is emitted through the documented structured channel, redacted diagnostics remain on stderr, and the documented non-zero exit is stable.

Given malformed JSON, unknown fields forbidden by the schema, or provider transport, mailbox, MIME, recipient, schedule, delivery, or acknowledgement fields, when invocation is attempted, then validation fails before any mutation.

Given a successful or expected-failure invocation, when stdout and stderr are inspected, then stderr contains no transaction description, raw evidence, full account or instrument identifier, credential, or serialized request or response body.

Given output is lost after a committed mutation, when the External Orchestrator retries the same Idempotency Identity and semantic input, then the original structured outcome is recoverable without duplicate effects.
security_criteria: Diagnostics and logs contain metadata only, never financial payloads, credentials, or full account identifiers.
